title: Re-enable message deletion, remove all caller checks, and make pinned videos per-user

requirements.summary: Re-enable message deletion on the admin page with backend function and UI delete button per message
acceptanceCriteria: A backend function exists to delete a message by ID with no caller/principal check
The admin page displays messages with a delete button per message
Clicking delete removes the message from the backend and updates the UI
No caller check is added to the delete function

file_operations.description: Add a new section to display flagged messages with individual delete buttons. Fetch flagged messages using getFlaggedMessages from the backend interface. For each message, render a delete button that calls the deleteMessage backend function with the message ID. Update the UI by refetching the flagged messages list after successful deletion. Include error handling for deletion failures.

Add a useGetFlaggedMessages query hook that calls actor.getFlaggedMessages() and returns an array of Message objects. Add a useDeleteMessage mutation hook that accepts a messageId (bigint) and calls actor.deleteMessage(messageId). The mutation should invalidate the flagged messages query cache on success to trigger a refetch. Both hooks should follow the existing query/mutation patterns in this file.

requirements.summary: Change pinned video storage to be per-user so each user only sees their own pinned videos
acceptanceCriteria: Pinning a video in a chatroom only affects the pinning user's view
Other users in the same chatroom do not see the pinned video unless they pinned it themselves
Unpinning a video only removes it for the user who unpinned it
The pinned video state is stored per (chatroomId, userId) in the backend
No caller checks are used — user identity is passed as an explicit parameter or derived from an anonymous/anonymous-safe mechanism consistent with the no-caller-check requirement

file_operations.description: Update the useGetPinnedVideo query hook to pass a userId parameter when calling actor.getPinnedVideo(chatroomId, userId). Derive the userId from the current user's profile or generate a stable anonymous identifier if no profile exists. Update the usePinVideo mutation hook to pass userId when calling actor.pinVideo(chatroomId, userId, messageId). Update the useUnpinVideo mutation hook to pass userId when calling actor.unpinVideo(chatroomId, userId). Ensure all three hooks consistently use the same userId derivation logic. Update query key dependencies to include userId so different users get separate cached pinned video states.

Update the pinned video fetching and rendering logic to pass the current user's userId to the useGetPinnedVideo hook. Ensure the userId is consistently derived from the user profile (via getCallerUserProfile) or from a stable anonymous identifier if not logged in. Update the pinVideo action to pass the userId. Verify that the pinned video only appears for the user who pinned it by confirming the query key includes userId.

Update the useUnpinVideo mutation call to pass the current user's userId as a parameter. Ensure the userId is derived consistently with the ChatArea component. Update any conditional rendering logic if needed to ensure the unpin button only appears when the current user is the one who pinned the video.

Update the pin button onClick handler to pass the current user's userId when calling the pinVideo mutation. Derive the userId consistently with other components (from user profile or anonymous identifier). Ensure the pin action is scoped to the current user only.
